title: ISic001140. Fragmentary funerary inscription
body:
Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa
1Α(VAC.1)Π
2ΚΛΞΚΒΥΩ
3ΘΖΝΛΩ
4ΕΓΕ(vac.1)ΥΒ

Text encoding: Victoria Fendel
ΑΠ (vac.1) ΚΛΞΚΒΥΩ ΘΖΝΛΩ ΕΓΕ (vac.1) ΥΒ

Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa
1Α  Π
2ΚΛΞΚΒΥΩ
3ΘΖΝΛΩ
4ΕΓΕ  ΥΒ

Text encoding: Victoria Fendel
ΑΠ  ΚΛΞΚΒΥΩΘΖΝΛΩΕΓΕ  ΥΒ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of IGTermini

translation

commentary

Brugnone (1974) notes that the vague details of discovery are insufficient to establish whether the this tombstone was related to the tomb near which it was found, or whether it was re-used. The text itself makes no sense. Brugnone (1974) wonders whether the text is intended to call to mind a funerary inscription with the dedication Th. K., and with an indication of the age of the deceased at the end. Brugnone posits that this text was a mason's exercise or the work of an apprentice.

bibliography

  • NSA 1879 254 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001392
  • Kaibel 1890 321 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001072
  • Brugnone 1974 19 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001397

Letters are very thin, and chiselling is rough and uneven. Letters are unevenly spaced. Small serifs on letters. Use of cursive omega; line 2 xi in plaster cast has a mid-line which slopes upwards; upsilon in lines 2 and 4 are shaped like a lower-case alphabetic 'Y'.

Line 1 24 Line 2 17-27 Line 3 20-31 Line 4 24-27 Interlineation line 1 to 2
